title: Bug: runFixture beads diff misses tasks created via BD_NO_DAEMON/MCP paths (flush race); rubric spec used naive strict assertion
description: Live attempt 3 (trace rubric-revision-loop-2026-06-11T21-45-00-465Z.jsonl) demonstrated the full relay loop (grader spawned at ROOT twice, grade-record invoked, Stop approved) but failed at beadsTasksCreated.length>0: the trace's beads diff is EMPTY despite two mcp bd_create_task calls + one Bash 'BD_NO_DAEMON=1 bd create' visible in toolCalls. Root cause hypothesis to verify offline with real bd: the fixture's issues.jsonl is not flushed from the DB before runFixture diffs it (BD_NO_DAEMON writes skip the daemon's auto-sync). Evidence: G8-era fixtures documented the same race and their specs use a defensive OR-shape (harness diff OR MCP tool call OR bash bd create); rubric-revision-loop.spec.ts:117 used the naive strict form. Fix: (1) runFixture flushes beads (bd sync/export in the fixture) before diffing; (2) spec adopts the established OR-shape; (3) offline L2-style test of the capture path with real bd; (4) offline re-validation of all spec assertions against the recorded trace.
notes:
COMPLETED: evidence-before-fix protocol fully executed.

Step 1 — REPRO CONFIRMED (offline, real bd 0.47.1):
  /tmp/l1r7-repro/demo with bd daemon active for the workspace:
  - PRE-FIX behavior: after daemon-route 'bd create' (no BD_NO_DAEMON), readBeadsIssues(fixture) yields size=0, beadsTasksCreated=[] — DESPITE the SQLite DB carrying the row.
  - POST-FIX behavior: after 'BD_NO_DAEMON=1 bd sync --flush-only', readBeadsIssues yields size=3, beadsTasksCreated=['demo-42f','demo-tus','demo-xvp'].
  The hypothesis is confirmed for the daemon path (bd_create_task via MCP). The BD_NO_DAEMON bash path auto-flushes synchronously, so the live trace's empty diff was multi-causal — but the harness-side fix (flush before pre and post snapshots) defends against ALL bd routing paths.

Step 2 — FAILING TEST FIRST:
  Added .claude/tests/e2e/specs/_beads-capture.unit.spec.ts encoding:
    - flush+diff captures BD_NO_DAEMON-route tasks (the contract that the race violated by reading without flushing).
    - flush is idempotent (3 repeated calls preserve state).
    - flushFixtureBeads tolerance: noBeadsDir, bdMissing both return structured failure instead of throwing.
  Pre-fix this spec would not have existed; under the new lib/beadsCapture.ts module it asserts the post-fix contract.
  Pre-fix the LIVE spec's failure was: 'AssertionError: expected 0 to be greater than 0 at line 117'.

Step 3 — FIX APPLIED:
  - .claude/tests/e2e/lib/beadsCapture.ts (NEW): readBeadsIssues, diffBeadsIssues, flushFixtureBeads helpers (BD_NO_DAEMON=1 bd sync --flush-only with cwd=fixturePath, prefers fixture's .claude/bin/bd shim if present, tolerant of missing .beads/, missing bd, non-zero exit).
  - .claude/tests/e2e/lib/runFixture.ts: imports the new module; flushes before BOTH the pre-run snapshot (line 787) and the post-run beadsAfter read (line 940); removed the now-duplicate readBeadsIssues + diffBeadsIssues definitions; flush failures log a warning to stderr and proceed (capture is best-effort).

Step 4 — SPEC OR-SHAPE:
  .claude/tests/e2e/specs/rubric-revision-loop.spec.ts:117 now uses the canonical OR-shape (harness diff OR MCP bd_create_task OR Bash bd create) verbatim from go-cli-refactor.spec.ts. Comment cross-references this bug task + that established pattern.

Step 5 — RECORDED-TRACE REGRESSION ANCHOR:
  - .claude/tests/e2e/cassettes/seed/rubric-revision-loop-2026-06-11T21-45-00-465Z.jsonl (NEW, committed): the Phase A live trace.
  - .claude/tests/e2e/.gitignore: allow cassettes/seed/ (cassettes/replays/ stays gitignored).
  - .claude/tests/e2e/specs/_phase-a-trace.unit.spec.ts (NEW): asserts against the recorded trace — grader fired x2 ROOT-parented, grade-record bash present, Stop fired, OR-shape passes, mcpCreateCount >= 2, bashBdCreateCount >= 1, qa + backend invocations present.

VERIFICATION:
  - make lint                                  -> pass (exit 0)
  - make test-all                              -> 20 specs / 392 assertions / 0 failed
  - cd .claude/tests/e2e && npm run test:unit  -> 113 tests / 2 skipped / 9 files / 0 failed
  - The new _beads-capture and _phase-a-trace specs both pass.

FILES (absolute paths):
  Created:
    /Users/edk0/Desktop/projects/claude-workflow-plugin/.claude/tests/e2e/lib/beadsCapture.ts
    /Users/edk0/Desktop/projects/claude-workflow-plugin/.claude/tests/e2e/specs/_beads-capture.unit.spec.ts
    /Users/edk0/Desktop/projects/claude-workflow-plugin/.claude/tests/e2e/specs/_phase-a-trace.unit.spec.ts
    /Users/edk0/Desktop/projects/claude-workflow-plugin/.claude/tests/e2e/cassettes/seed/rubric-revision-loop-2026-06-11T21-45-00-465Z.jsonl
  Modified:
    /Users/edk0/Desktop/projects/claude-workflow-plugin/.claude/tests/e2e/lib/runFixture.ts
    /Users/edk0/Desktop/projects/claude-workflow-plugin/.claude/tests/e2e/specs/rubric-revision-loop.spec.ts
    /Users/edk0/Desktop/projects/claude-workflow-plugin/.claude/tests/e2e/.gitignore

KEY DECISIONS:
  - Used 'bd sync --flush-only' (not bd export) because it's the targeted in-place flush operation per bd --help; bd export writes to stdout or a chosen file.
  - Set BD_NO_DAEMON=1 on the flush itself so it can't be enqueued on the daemon (otherwise the fix would have the same race it's fixing).
  - Best-effort failure model: log to stderr, proceed. The spec OR-shape carries the structural fallback so a flush failure (CI without bd, lock contention, corrupt DB) doesn't fail a spec.
  - Capture trace cassettes/seed/ as committed evidence (not replays/) so the offline anchor doesn't bit-rot when replays/ gets cleaned.
  - Did NOT add qa-approved label; this is for QA to decide.

IN PROGRESS: None — ready for QA.

QA NOTES:
  - The pre-fix race is conditional on the bd daemon being active for the fixture's workspace. The offline repro above shows it clearly.
  - The fix is conservative: it adds a synchronous flush at two well-defined points; it does not change beads behavior or skip any error path. Specs that DON'T use beads are unaffected (noBeadsDir branch returns ok:true).
  - The OR-shape change in rubric-revision-loop.spec.ts is the same pattern from 4 other specs (go-cli-refactor, monorepo-frontend-only, python-django-bug, qa-block-recovery) — it's a known-good defense pattern.
  - The _phase-a-trace.unit.spec.ts anchor is the regression-bar artifact. If a future change to the workflow breaks the rubric loop shape, this offline test will fail before anyone re-runs live.
close_reason: qa-approved; flush fix + OR-shape + seed anchor

title: G2.model-spawn: model-select adopted claude-fable-5 which fails subagent spawning intermittently (5 spawns OK then 4 failed same session); resolver validates listing-resolution only (spec 0.3), not sustained spawnability

During gauntlet, agents llh.1/2/4/6 spawned successfully on fable-5 pins; llh.3/7/8/13 then failed with 'issue with the selected model (claude-fable-5). It may not exist or you may not have access to it.' Intermittent access. Re-pinned to claude-opus-4-7 (proven spawnable across all prior phases) to unblock. IMPACT: vlp Stage-1 claim 'selects newest model automatically' is PROVEN-WITH-CAVEAT — resolver picks listing-resolvable models but never validates sustained subagent-spawn capability. MECHANICAL FIX OPTIONS (evidence-before-fix, do not patch yet): (a) resolver spawn-probe validation before adopting; (b) ranking exclusion of models with unstable spawn access; (c) fallback chain on spawn failure. comments.author: username
comments.text: ROOT CAUSE SHARPENED: re-pinning agent .md frontmatter to claude-opus-4-7 did NOT unblock subagent spawns this session — they still failed instantly on claude-fable-5 (0 tool calls). The harness caches agent definitions at SESSION START; mid-session file rewrites don't take effect until restart. Two consequences for the model-select feature: (1) its whole mechanism (SessionStart rewrites pins) only affects the NEXT session, so a freshly-adopted model is never validated in the session that adopts it; (2) if the cached pin's model loses spawn access mid-session, ALL subagent spawns brick until session restart OR an explicit per-spawn model override. Unblocked this session by passing the Agent tool's model='opus' override (takes precedence over cached frontmatter). This strengthens the mechanical-fix need: spawn-probe validation must run in the adopting session, and there should be a documented per-spawn override escape.

comments.author: username
comments.text:
<!-- BD-MCP-META: {"kind":"evidence","source":"qa-agent","phase":"before-fix"} -->
## Evidence-before-fix (root cause verified)

**Old check (`invQaQueriedImpactOf`, invariants.ts ~538-630):** walks QA `parentToolUseId` chains for tool_use names matching `/code-graph.*impact_of|^impact_of$/` and FAILs when fewer than `min_calls` (default 1) are QA-attributable, given code-graph present + fileWrites>0.

**Why it's wrong (n6d, llh.2):** `qa-gate.sh enter` now invokes `.claude/scripts/impact-report.sh`, which drives the code-graph server over raw stdio JSON-RPC (one process, FIFO-held stdin) — those `impact_of` calls are NOT Claude tool_uses, so they never appear in `trace.toolCalls`. `qa-gate.sh approve` REFUSES (exit 2) without a fresh artifact (`change_set_hash` match), bypassable only via `--no-impact-report '<reason>'`. The artifact lives at `.claude/.qa-tracking/impact-report-<taskid>.json` with shape `{generated_at, task_id, change_set_hash, files:[{file,impact}], server:"code-graph"|"absent"}`. Net: a correct n6d live run produces 0 QA-attributable `impact_of` tool_uses → old invariant FAILS by construction. This blocks the Stage-2 paid pass.

**Trace forensics (offline, all existing cassettes):**
- `trace.toolCalls` records Bash `input.command` strings, Read `input.file_path`, and `subagentInvocations` with QA `toolUseId` for parent-chain attribution. Verified the trace captures `qa-gate.sh enter`/`approve` Bash commands and is queryable for `--no-impact-report` and the artifact path.
- The 3 pre-n6d anchor seed traces (`node-react-auth-2026-06-11T23-34-49-784Z`, `...2026-06-12T00-50-56-312Z`, `...2026-06-12T02-18-12-871Z`) ALL: have 7 code-graph tools in `toolsAvailable`, fileWrites 7-9, QA-attributable `qa-gate.sh enter`+`approve` calls — but **0** `impact_of` tool_uses, **0** `impact-report.sh` invocations, **0** references to `impact-report` anywhere, and **0** `--no-impact-report` flags.
- **Timeline:** `impact-report.sh` / `--no-impact-report` were created 2026-06-13 (llh.2). Every existing trace is dated 2026-06-11/06-12 → ALL pre-n6d. There is NO existing live n6d trace; synthetic traces are the only model (matches "offline; synthetic traces only").

**Design consequence (revises the prompt's framing):** pre-n6d traces do NOT "lack the enter signature" — they HAVE enter+approve-unbypassed. So enter-present / approve-unbypassed ALONE cannot be the PASS signal (it would make pre-n6d anchors PASS, not SKIP). The reliable post-n6d discriminator is the **n6d artifact footprint**: (i) a QA-attributable reference to the `impact-report-<taskid>.json` path (qa.md's mandated FIRST ACTION is `cat .claude/.qa-tracking/impact-report-<task-id>.json`) or an `impact-report.sh` invocation, and (ii) the `--no-impact-report` flag — both post-n6d-only constructs absent from every pre-n6d trace.

**Chosen signature** (strongest combination observable in the trace):
- FAIL if a QA-attributable `qa-gate.sh approve … --no-impact-report <reason>` with a non-code-graph-absent reason exists → analysis genuinely bypassed (post-n6d-only construct, so unambiguous).
- PASS if (no disqualifying bypass) AND a QA-attributable n6d artifact footprint exists (cat/Read of `impact-report-*.json`, or `impact-report.sh` invocation) AND a QA-attributable `qa-gate.sh approve` for the task with no bypass → the artifact was consulted and the unbypassed approve mechanically proves it existed & was fresh (the refusal enforces it).
- SKIP "code-graph absent" if no code-graph in toolsAvailable, OR the footprint shows `server:"absent"`, OR bypass reason names code-graph absence.
- SKIP vacuous if fileWrites==0.
- SKIP "pre-n6d recording" if code-graph present + fileWrites>0 but NO artifact footprint AND NO bypass flag (the mechanic didn't exist when recorded) → mirrors the llh.4 pre-3.5 skip; the 3 anchors land here.

META-TEST: PASS trace with the artifact-footprint signal removed → flips to SKIP-pre-n6d / FAIL (loud), proving the signal is load-bearing.

comments.text:
<!-- BD-MCP-META: {"kind":"verdict","source":"qa-agent","phase":"after-fix"} -->
## QA VERDICT: APPROVED (faithful, not a goalpost-move)

Reconstructed the evidence-before-fix the dead agent skipped, plus independent adversarial scrutiny.

**Faithful rewrite (failing-first reconstructed independently):** Copied the OLD invQaQueriedImpactOf (impact_of-counting, git HEAD) verbatim into a throwaway spec and ran it against the new n6d-positive synthetic trace -> FAILS ("0 impact_of call(s) from QA, expected at least 1"). NEW invariant PASSES non-skip on the same trace. The rewrite genuinely changed behavior; the signal it keys on (artifact footprint + --no-impact-report) is absent from every pre-n6d trace.

**Anchors honest:** run3/run4 seed traces have 0 impact-report references, 0 impact_of tool_uses, 0 --no-impact-report flags, and are dated 2026-06-11/12 (impact-report.sh created 2026-06-13). FAIL->SKIP "pre-n6d recording" is correct (mirrors llh.4 pre-3.5 skip), not a dodge. Anchor specs pass.

**Live-run linchpin (qa.md S3a):** §3a line 154 mandates "FIRST ACTION when any files have changed: ... read the mechanical impact report at .claude/.qa-tracking/impact-report-<task-id>.json"; line 160 shows the literal `cat .claude/.qa-tracking/impact-report-<task-id>.json`. So a faithful live run produces the QA-attributable footprint AND a real unbypassed qa-gate.sh approve -> Branch A PASS (invariants.ts:691). The paid run yields PROVEN, not SKIP.

**Mechanical floor verified:** qa-gate.sh cmd_approve (lines ~612-648) genuinely exits 2 on missing/invalid/stale/unverifiable impact-report-<task-id>.json. The unbypassed approve cannot succeed on a live run without a fresh artifact, so the qa-approved label (and Stop release) is downstream of real enforcement. The floor lives in the runtime script (correct n6d "mechanics over prompts" design), not the post-hoc invariant.

**Attribution sound:** a footprint+approve attributed to backend (not QA) does NOT pass the positive branch (falls through to pre-n6d SKIP). isQaAttributable requires QA parentage.

**Bypass->FAIL not gameable in the lax direction:** probed 12 reasons against codeGraphAbsentReason; "in a hurry"/"no time"/"skip it" all correctly classify as non-degradation -> FAIL. The one miss ("the code graph index is missing") is a false-negative-toward-FAIL (stricter), the safe direction.

**NON-BLOCKING weakness (follow-up recommended, NOT a goalpost-move):** Branch B footprint-alone (invariants.ts:705) PASSES on a loose string match — verified `echo "...impact-report-X.json"` and `git log | grep impact-report.sh` (no real consultation) trip it, and the detail line over-claims "QA consulted... impact analysis ran". The Trace schema (lib/trace.ts ToolCall) records no per-call exit code, so the invariant cannot observe approve success and infers it from presence — "strongest checkable form" given the trace constraint, and Branch B does NOT fire on a normal live run (which records the approve in QA scope -> Branch A). Acceptable for ship; recommend a follow-up to require the unbypassed-approve co-signal for the PASS (drop or downgrade footprint-alone to SKIP) so a hand-built trace cannot green on a mention.

**Suites:** tsc --noEmit clean except the pre-existing _lib.unit.spec.ts(136,34) error. npm run test:unit = 15 files / 343 pass / 5 skip. make test-all = 607 L2 assertions / 22 specs / 0 fail. New _invariants block = 59 tests pass incl. the load-bearing footprint META-TEST and the footprint-present-but-bypassed FAIL.

Approved via qa-gate.sh approve --no-impact-report (gauntlet e2e-invariant task editing the test harness; impact-report mechanism itself proven by llh.2; rubric override: e2e-invariant task, rubric workflow N/A).

comments.author: username
comments.text:
QA-GATE work note (specialist, gate-entered).

FIX (Branch B tightening; load-bearing proven RED→GREEN):
- Root cause confirmed: invariants.ts Branch B (`if (qaArtifactFootprint)` footprint-ALONE → PASS) greened on a loose string match. artifactPattern (/impact-report-…\.json|impact-report\.sh/) fires on ANY command text mentioning the path, so a hand-built `echo`/`grep` mention with NO approve tripped it.
- Fix: downgraded Branch B from PASS to SKIP ("skipped: consultation footprint present … but no in-scope unbypassed qa-gate.sh approve to confirm it"). The ONLY defensible PASS is now Branch A (footprint + unbypassed approve), because qa-gate.sh approve REFUSES without a fresh artifact — that refusal is the co-signal a bare mention lacks. SKIP (not FAIL) because the approve may simply be outside the captured QA scope.
- branch_b_change: `pass:true` → `pass:true, skipped:true` with the new detail. Branch A (line ~691, footprint+unbypassed approve → PASS) untouched. Pre-n6d fallthrough untouched.
- overmatch_now_skips: TRUE. Proved by reconstructing the pre-fix Branch B in a scratch engine copy: the over-match trace (QA echoes impact-report-*.json, no approve) returned PASS pre-fix and SKIP post-fix.
- Also updated the function docstring with a new "SKIP footprint without approve (llh.16)" entry.

SPEC (.claude/tests/e2e/specs/_invariants.unit.spec.ts):
- OVER-MATCH (llh.16): echo/grep mention of impact-report-*.json + NO approve → SKIP, not PASS; asserts evaluateAll surfaces it in `skipped`, not `failed`.
- POSITIVE stays PASS (llh.16): footprint + unbypassed approve still greens via Branch A (legit live signature unaffected).
- META-TEST (llh.16): remove ONLY the unbypassed approve from the live PASS trace → drops PASS to SKIP (the co-signal is load-bearing).
- 62 tests pass (59 baseline + 3 new).

ANCHORS re-confirmed (direct qa-queried-impact-of eval over latest replay per fixture):
- Pre-n6d anchors (May: go-cli-refactor, monorepo-frontend-only, multi-domain-signup, python-django-bug, qa-block-recovery, rubric-revision-loop) → all SKIP (code-graph absent). Unchanged.
- node-react-auth latest (June, 2026-06-13T16-52) → PASS via Branch A (verified: code-graph present, fileWrites=2, QA-attributable artifact footprint + unbypassed approve). This is a genuine post-n6d live run, NOT a pre-n6d anchor; PASS is correct and unchanged by the Branch B edit.

Trace-schema note (root constraint, unaddressed by design): the trace still lacks a per-call exit code, so a footprint that is genuinely a failed `cat` cannot be distinguished from a successful one at the footprint layer — which is exactly why footprint-alone is now SKIP rather than PASS.

Verify: _invariants.unit.spec.ts 62/62. NOT approving; no commit.

title: Gate-friction case-3 residual: live test-live runs transiently mutate e2e fixture scripts (llh.8 run-start sync), false-blocking the ORCHESTRATOR's own Stop gate with a fixture-only git-diff while changed-files.txt is empty
description: Observed 5x across sessions: during make test-live, runFixture syncs canonical scripts into fixtures/<f>/.claude/scripts/ and the live run mutates them; the orchestrator session's Stop hook sees raw git-diff churn under .claude/tests/e2e/fixtures/*/ (changed_files=[] post-denylist, but diff_summary non-empty) and blocks 'QA approval required'. Fix: denylist .claude/tests/e2e/fixtures/*/.claude/{scripts,beads}/ transient churn in verify-before-stop.sh's gate path (extends llh.3). These are test fixtures, not orchestrator deliverables. Land after the paid pass; the running fixture restores itself on completion.

comments.author: username
comments.text:
QA-GATE work note (specialist, gate-entered).

FIX (evidence-before-fix; repro RED → fix → GREEN):
- Root cause confirmed: during `make test-live`, runFixture syncs canonical hook scripts into fixtures/<f>/.claude/scripts/ (llh.8 run-start sync) and the live run mutates the fixture's own .beads/ ledger. The orchestrator session's Stop hook fires with changed-files.txt EMPTY, so it hits the git-status fallback (vbs ~lines 578-608), which keeps any path passing is_tracked_change. The denylist did NOT exclude fixture-internal .claude/scripts/ or .beads/, so the gate false-blocked "QA approval required" on harness-internal transient churn.
- Verified actual fixture layout: each fixture has `.beads` at the fixture ROOT (fixtures/<f>/.beads/), and `.claude/scripts` under it. (Task wording said `.claude/{scripts,beads}` but real beads is `.beads`; regex covers both the real `.beads` and a defensive `.claude/beads`.)
- Fix: extended DENYLIST_REGEX (vbs line 116) with one alternative:
  `(^|/)\.claude/tests/e2e/fixtures/[^/]+/(\.claude/(scripts|beads)|\.beads)/`
  Placed alongside the existing `(^|/)\.claude/worktrees/` alternative. Scoped to the fixtures' scripts/beads subtrees only.
- Repro RED→GREEN proven via the spec META "with fixtures denylist stripped, fixture-script churn BLOCKS": a copy of vbs with the alternative removed blocks the churn; the canonical (fixed) script allows it.
- Realistic composite check (separate, manual): fixture script churn + project-root .beads churn + no active task → ALLOW (fixture churn denylisted; project-root .beads handled by the existing beads-state fast-path classifier, which is intentionally left intact for audit-trail).

SPEC (failing-first, drives the GIT-STATUS FALLBACK specifically — distinct from llh.3 repro-3 which used TRACKING_FILE): added a vbs-llh17 block to .claude/tests/component/specs/verify-before-stop.sh with a nested e2e-fixture tree in a git fixture:
- Repro 1: fixture-internal script churn (git fallback) → ALLOW
- Repro 2: fixture-internal .beads churn (git fallback) → ALLOW
- ANTI-OVERREACH 1: a REAL plugin-source change (src/real-source.ts) → still BLOCK
- ANTI-OVERREACH 2: a fixture DELIVERABLE (fixture.yaml, NOT under scripts/beads) → still BLOCK
- META x2: strip the fixtures alternative from a copy → fixture churn BLOCKS again (load-bearing).

NOTE on project-root `.beads/`: left OUT of the denylist deliberately (audit-trail-relevant; handled by is_fastpath_only_change). Only fixture-internal churn is denylisted. Comment block at vbs ~line 99 documents the anti-overreach scoping.

Verify: verify-before-stop component spec green at 74 assertions (65 baseline + 3 llh.20 + 6 llh.17). shellcheck clean. NOT approving; no commit.

title: P0: Stop gate trusts the forgeable qa-approved LABEL, not a tamper-evident approval bound to the change-set — bare 'bd label add qa-approved' (or a decoy-task current-task redirect) releases unreviewed code
description: RED-TEAM FALSIFICATION (P0+P1, unified fix). P0: verify-before-stop.sh:982-992 releases on GATE_STATUS==approved == has_label(qa-approved); 'bd label add <task> qa-approved' (every agent has bd) sets it WITHOUT the impact-report refusal / audit comment / rubric that qa-gate.sh approve enforces. P1: the gate checks approval of whatever current-task points at (vbs:563,986) and NEVER binds it to the tracked changed files — approve a trivial decoy, current-task.sh set <decoy>, ship unrelated unreviewed code. MECHANICAL FIX (evidence-before-fix): qa-gate.sh approve writes a tamper-evident approval record carrying change_set_hash (the impact-report's sha of sorted changed-files); verify-before-stop.sh recomputes the current change-set hash and requires a matching QA-GATE-APPROVED record, not merely the label. A bare label-add writes no record→block; a decoy approval has a different hash→block; post-approval edits change the hash→re-block (also closes a staleness hole). Breaks ledger R4/R3/P5/S4/I2/I11. Repro in red-team report (task output).

comments.author: username
comments.text:
## G1 EVIDENCE — repro captured RED (evidence-before-fix, spec 0.5)

Sandbox: git+beads init, real source change to `src/app.ts` tracked in `changed-files.txt`, gate entered + current-task set. All driving the REAL plugin scripts (symlinked).

**Baseline (control):** unreviewed `src/app.ts` change → `verify-before-stop.sh` stdin `{"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_hook_active":false}` → **decision=block**. Correct.

**P0 — forged bare label (CONFIRMED red):**
- An agent (every agent has `bd`) runs `bd label add <task> qa-approved` — NO `qa-gate.sh approve`.
- `qa-gate.sh status` → `approved` (because status == `has_label qa-approved`).
- `verify-before-stop.sh` → **decision=ALLOW**. Unreviewed code released.
- Sidesteps the n6d impact-report refusal, the audit comment, and the rubric — all of which `qa-gate.sh approve` enforces but a bare label-add skips entirely.

**P1 — decoy-task redirect (CONFIRMED red):**
- Real unreviewed change blocks (control).
- Legitimately `qa-gate.sh approve` a TRIVIAL DECOY task (its change-set was `docs/trivial.md`).
- `current-task.sh set <decoy>`, then restore the REAL unreviewed change-set (`src/app.ts`) into `changed-files.txt`.
- `verify-before-stop.sh` → **decision=ALLOW**. The gate released unrelated, unreviewed `src/app.ts` under the decoy's approval.

**ROOT CAUSE = trust-boundary inversion.** The Stop hook's release predicate is `qa-gate.sh status == approved`, which reduces to `has_label qa-approved` (vbs ~982-992). The label is:
1. **Forgeable** — settable by any agent via a bare `bd label add`, bypassing the `qa-gate.sh approve` code path (P0). The gate trusts a token that the thing-being-gated can mint.
2. **Unbound to the change-set** — the gate checks approval of whatever `current-task.sh get` returns (vbs ~563, ~986) and never binds that approval to the tracked changed files (P1). Approval is a property of a task-id, not of the bytes shipping.

**FIX DIRECTION (next):** bind approval to the change-set via a tamper-evident record carrying `change_set_hash`. Reuse the n6d canonical hash — `impact-report.sh --hash-only` (verified deterministic + sensitive: same changed-files.txt → identical sha256; adding a file → different sha256). `qa-gate.sh approve` writes a `QA-GATE APPROVED change_set_hash=<h>` record; `verify-before-stop.sh` recomputes the current hash and requires a matching record, not bare label presence. Blocks P0 (no record), P1 (record hash ≠ current change-set), and post-approval edits (hash drift). Residual: an agent with arbitrary shell can still forge the comment itself — to be documented, NOT overclaimed as tamper-proof.

comments.author: username
comments.text:
## FIX IMPLEMENTED + FLIP EVIDENCE (spec 0.5: repro → root cause → fix → flip → residual)

### Root cause (recap)
Trust-boundary inversion: the Stop hook released on `qa-gate.sh status == approved`, which reduces to `has_label qa-approved` — a token (a) any agent can mint via a bare `bd label add` (P0), and (b) bound to a task-id, never to the bytes shipping (P1).

### Fix mechanism (change-set-bound, tamper-evident record; reuses n6d machinery)
**`qa-gate.sh approve`** (`.claude/scripts/qa-gate.sh`):
- Added shared helper `compute_change_set_hash()` that delegates to the ONE canonical implementation — `impact-report.sh --hash-only` (sha256 of the sorted, denylist-filtered changed-files list). No 4th copy of the denylist; the refusal block now also calls this helper.
- After setting `qa-approved`, approve writes the canonical hash INTO the approval comment: `QA-GATE APPROVED change_set_hash=<h> at <ts>: <summary>`. Computed OUTSIDE the IMPACT-REPORT-REFUSAL sentinel block so (a) the `--no-impact-report` bypass path still binds, and (b) the n6d META-strip stays coherent (verified: n6d strip META still green). Empty hash → degrades to legacy unbound comment + sync-errors.log warning (labels remain the lifecycle source of truth; approval is not aborted).

**`verify-before-stop.sh`** (`.claude/scripts/verify-before-stop.sh`):
- Added `current_change_set_hash()` (shares `impact-report.sh --hash-only` — no denylist duplication) and `task_has_matching_approval_record <tid> <hash>` (reads back `QA-GATE APPROVED ...change_set_hash=<h>` comments via `bd show --json`, exact-matches the token).
- Approved-path release now requires BOTH `GATE_STATUS == approved` AND a matching change-set-bound record. Label-present-but-no-matching-record → `LABEL_WITHOUT_RECORD=true` → BLOCK with the exact reason: *"qa-approved label present but no change-set-bound approval record matches the current changes — approve via qa-gate.sh approve, not a bare label add"* plus a 3-cause explanation (forged label / decoy redirect / post-approval edit) and the correct enter→impact-report→approve remediation.
- Fail-CLOSED: an unverifiable current hash (impact-report.sh missing) blocks rather than passes.

### Preserved (verified byte-stable / green)
qa-deferred audited escape, F1/beads/empty fast-paths (llh.3 — fast-path exits before the approved-path check, so doc-only/beads auto-approve is untouched), escalation state machine (escalation-binding 51 assertions green), the n6d impact-report refusal + bypass, the 0wk.2 baseline/truncate logic. qa-approved label remains necessary; the hash-bound record is now ADDITIONALLY required.

### Flip evidence (same sandbox as the G1 repro, now against the FIXED scripts)
| Scenario | Pre-fix | Post-fix |
|---|---|---|
| control (entered, unapproved) | block | block |
| **P0 forged bare label** | **ALLOW** | **block** ✅ |
| legit `qa-gate.sh approve` | (n/a) | **ALLOW** ✅ (release still works) |
| post-approval edit (hash drift) | ALLOW | **block** ✅ (bonus: staleness hole closed) |
| **P1 decoy-task redirect** | **ALLOW** | **block** ✅ |

### Tests (failing-first; security-critical)
- **verify-before-stop.sh spec** 45 → 61 (+16): P0 forged-label-blocks, P1 decoy-redirect-blocks, positive legit-release, recorded-hash==--hash-only, hash-mismatch re-block, + META-TEST that shadows `task_has_matching_approval_record` to always-match and asserts the forged label then RELEASES (proves the check is load-bearing — the P0 assertion would fail without it).
- **qa-gate.sh spec** 68 → 74 (+6): approve writes a change_set_hash record == `--hash-only`; bypass path still binds.
- **escalation-binding** META line ref 921→974 (guard physically moved; count stays 51). **failure-hook-crash** fixture reordered to seed the change-set before approve (its old approve-then-seed pattern is, under the fix, correctly a post-approval edit → block; the real flow always reviews the actual files). vbs/qa-gate META line refs (829→882, 1016→1131, 396→408) updated for the shift.
- Full `make test-all`: **22 specs, 629 assertions, all green** (was 607; +22). `make test` (L1): 16/16. `make lint`: clean.

### Documented residual threat boundary (NOT overclaimed)
An agent with arbitrary shell + the hash tool can still forge the comment itself: `bd comment add "QA-GATE APPROVED change_set_hash=$(impact-report.sh --hash-only)"`. The fix raises the bar from "trivially-forgeable label" to "must reproduce a change-set-bound record", defeats accidental / lazy / bare-`bd label add` drift, and binds approval to the reviewed files (P1) — but is NOT a cryptographic sandbox. A signed/HMAC'd record keyed on a secret withheld from the gated process would be required for that, which the full-shell-autonomy model precludes. This boundary is stated in the script comments at both the producer (qa-gate.sh approve) and consumer (vbs block reason) sites.

NOT approving (per task instructions); no commits.

comments.author: username
comments.text:
QA-GATE BLOCKED at 2026-06-13T18:05:40Z: QA BLOCK (P0 security centerpiece). Independently reproduced all 6 mandate scenarios in a from-scratch git+beads sandbox driving the real (uncommitted) scripts, code-graph absent. CONFIRMED GOOD (genuine pre/post flip proven): P0 forged bare-label -> BLOCK (pre-fix ALLOWed); P1 decoy redirect -> BLOCK; positive legit approve -> RELEASE (works code-graph-absent); post-approval edit (hash drift) -> re-BLOCK; residual hand-forged comment is REAL and HONESTLY documented (producer THREAT-MODEL BOUNDARY comment + consumer block reason + bd note 349, not overclaimed); suite green 22 specs/629 assertions, lint clean, META-test load-bearing.

BLOCKING DEFECT — fail-OPEN regression contradicting mandate point 5. verify-before-stop.sh:1060 `CURRENT_CS_HASH=$(current_change_set_hash)` runs under `set -e` (line 25). When impact-report.sh is MISSING (not merely failing), current_change_set_hash() executes `[ -f ... ] || { printf ""; return 1; }` and returns 1; the command-substitution assignment then trips set -e and ABORTS the whole script -> empty stdout, exit 1. Per the hooks spec only exit 2 / JSON decision:block blocks a Stop; exit 1 is a non-blocking error -> Claude ALLOWS THE STOP (fail-OPEN). The intended-fail-closed dead-code branch at 1069-1071 ("could not be recomputed (impact-report.sh missing/failing)") is UNREACHABLE in this sub-case. 

Asymmetry proving the bug: the PRESENT-BUT-FAILING case fails-CLOSED correctly (functions `|| printf ""` -> rc 0 -> LABEL_WITHOUT_RECORD block fires); only the MISSING-script case fails open. The producer qa-gate.sh approve correctly REFUSES (exit 2) when impact-report.sh is missing -> producer/consumer inconsistency. Pre-fix verify-before-stop.sh did NOT have this crash surface (it never called a return-1-on-missing fn in a set -e assignment at Stop time) -> this is a regression introduced by llh.18.

Exploit (re-opens the very P0 being fixed, one line): agent with shell runs `rm .claude/scripts/impact-report.sh` then bare `bd label add <task> qa-approved` -> Stop fails open -> unreviewed code ships. Also breaks legitimate gating in any code-graph-absent / partial-install env where impact-report.sh is absent: a real post-approval edit then fails to re-block.

FIX (one line, verified to flip missing-script case to exit 0 / decision=block = fail-CLOSED while leaving all 629 assertions intact): change vbs:1060 to `CURRENT_CS_HASH=$(current_change_set_hash) || true` (or `$(current_change_set_hash || true)`). Then add a failing-first spec for the MISSING-impact-report.sh case (current qa-gate + vbs specs only cover present/present-but-failing) asserting decision=block, and verify the META boundary. Re-enter for re-review after.

comments.author: username
comments.text:
## RE-WORK of QA BLOCK (bd note 355) — fail-OPEN on MISSING impact-report.sh — fixed + failing-first spec. NOT approving; left gate-entered for re-QA.

Spec 0.5 trail (repro → root cause → fix → flip → audit). Surface: `.claude/scripts/verify-before-stop.sh` + `.claude/tests/component/specs/verify-before-stop.sh` only. No commits, no paid runs.

### 1. REPRO captured RED (failing-first)
Added an L2 spec block `vbs-llh18-miss` (fresh git+beads fixture, empty test_cmd so the test pass is skipped, real plugin scripts symlinked). Builds a LEGIT change-set-bound approval (label + matching `QA-GATE APPROVED change_set_hash=<h>` record, hash == `impact-report.sh --hash-only`), then changes exactly ONE variable: removes `impact-report.sh`.

PRE-FIX run (`run.sh --filter verify-before-stop`): **pass=63 fail=2** —
- PASS sanity: with impact-report.sh PRESENT the matching record RELEASES (isolates the variable to script-presence).
- PASS precondition: impact-report.sh absent.
- **FAIL** `MISSING impact-report.sh -> FAIL-CLOSED (decision:block)` — the harness `miss_decision` returned `ABORT` (empty stdout + non-zero exit), NOT `block`.
- **FAIL** block-reason check — no reason at all (script aborted before emitting).

### 2. ROOT CAUSE = `set -e` abort on a bare command-substitution assignment
`verify-before-stop.sh` line 25 `set -e`. At the approved-path check, `CURRENT_CS_HASH=$(current_change_set_hash)`. `current_change_set_hash()` does `[ -f "$IMPACT_REPORT_SCRIPT" ] || { printf ''; return 1; }` → returns **1** when the script is MISSING. A bare `X=$(cmd)` whose RHS exits non-zero trips `set -e` → the whole script ABORTS with empty stdout + exit 1. Per the hooks contract only exit 2 / a JSON `decision:block` blocks a Stop; **exit 1 + empty stdout is NON-blocking → Claude ALLOWS the Stop (FAIL-OPEN)**. The implementer's intended fail-closed branch (`if [ -z "$CURRENT_CS_HASH" ]` arm of `LABEL_WITHOUT_RECORD`) was UNREACHABLE in this sub-case — the abort happens at the assignment, before the `if`.
Empirically confirmed in isolation: `bash -c 'set -e; f(){ printf ""; return 1; }; X=$(f); echo after'` → prints nothing, exit 1; with `X=$(f) || true` → continues, rc 0, X empty.
ASYMMETRY proving it's exactly this: the PRESENT-BUT-FAILING case fails CLOSED correctly (function body ends `|| printf ''` → rc 0 → empty hash → LABEL_WITHOUT_RECORD block fires). Only MISSING (no `|| ...` guard at the call site) aborts. Producer/consumer inconsistency too: `qa-gate.sh approve` REFUSES (exit 2) when impact-report.sh is missing, while the consumer fails open.

### 3. FIX (QA-verified one-liner, applied)
`verify-before-stop.sh` call site → `CURRENT_CS_HASH=$(current_change_set_hash) || true`. The assignment now yields rc 0 + empty hash on a missing script, so the missing-script case falls into the existing fail-CLOSED `LABEL_WITHOUT_RECORD` branch (its `[ -z "$CURRENT_CS_HASH" ]` arm → "could not be recomputed (impact-report.sh missing/failing)" → `emit_block`). Load-bearing-comment added at the site explaining the `set -e` semantics.

### 4. AUDIT for other `$(...)`-under-`set -e` fail-open instances in the approved-path block
`set -o pipefail` is NOT set → a pipe's rc is its last element only, so the `... | jq ... || echo` and `... | head -15` forms are all rc-0-safe. Of every command-sub assignment between the approved-path check and EOF, exactly ONE other had a bare (unguarded) form: `INTENT_JSON=$(compute_intent_payload)` (QA-required BLOCK path). `compute_intent_payload` ends in a bare `jq -nc ...`; with literal args it won't realistically fail, but it is the same anti-pattern on the block-emission path — a `jq` failure would abort mid-block → fail open. Hardened defensively: `INTENT_JSON=$(compute_intent_payload) || INTENT_JSON='{}'` (fallback keeps the block firing with a valid empty payload). Noted as defensive hardening, NOT a second active exploit (it falls through to a fail-closed default, unlike the P0 line). No other bare instances found.

### 5. FLIP + full verification (green post-fix)
| Scenario | Pre-fix | Post-fix |
|---|---|---|
| MISSING impact-report.sh (legit approval) | ABORT → ALLOW (fail-open) | **decision:block** ✅ (fail-closed) |
| block reason names "could not be recomputed" | (none — aborted) | **present** ✅ |
| present-but-failing impact-report.sh | block | **block** ✅ (unchanged) |
| positive legit release (script present) | ALLOW | **ALLOW** ✅ (unchanged) |

- `vbs-llh18-miss` (4 new assertions): all GREEN post-fix.
- verify-before-stop spec: 61 → **65** assertions, 0 fail.
- `make test-all`: 22 specs, **633** assertions (629 + 4 new), 0 fail.
- `make test` (L1): 16/16. `make lint`: clean.
- **escalation-binding byte-stable**: 51 assertions green; the `SUITE_REUSED" = "true"` guard the META anchors on is STILL at line 974 (my edits were below it).
- Original llh.18 scenarios re-confirmed green: P0 forged-label→BLOCK, P1 decoy-redirect→BLOCK, positive legit approve→RELEASE, post-approval-edit (hash drift)→re-BLOCK, META-llh18 (hash-match check load-bearing).

### 6. Test-infra note (line-drift maintenance)
My two added comment blocks shifted the `CHANGE_COUNT" -gt 15` truncation guard 1131→1153, which broke the truncation META-TEST's `NR==1131` absolute-line anchor. Re-anchored that META-TEST to the guard's UNIQUE TEXT pattern (`/CHANGE_COUNT" -gt 15/`) instead of an absolute line number — strictly more robust, identical force, and prevents the same brittle breakage on every future edit above the guard. The escalation-binding (`NR==974`) and lint-guard (`NR==882`) anchors were below/above my edits respectively and needed no change.

### Residual (unchanged from note 349, still honestly documented)
This binds approval to the reviewed files and defeats forged/stale labels and the missing-script fail-open, but is NOT a cryptographic sandbox against an adversary with arbitrary shell who reproduces the record (or the hash) by hand. Stated at both producer and consumer sites.

NOT approving (per instructions). Labels left `qa-blocked, qa-gate-entered` for re-QA.

Independently reproduced all mandate scenarios in a from-scratch git+beads sandbox driving the REAL uncommitted scripts, code-graph absent. (1) FAIL-OPEN CLOSED: legit-approved task + removed impact-report.sh -> decision:block (rc0), NOT abort/allow — the exact note-355 regression is reproduced as fixed; vbs:1075 CURRENT_CS_HASH=$(current_change_set_hash)||true routes the missing-script case into the fail-closed LABEL_WITHOUT_RECORD branch ("could not be recomputed"). (2) All originals hold: P0 forged bare label->BLOCK (reason names mechanism + steers to qa-gate.sh approve), P1 decoy redirect->BLOCK, legit approve->RELEASE, post-approval edit->re-BLOCK, present-but-failing IR->BLOCK (fail-closed). Drove the FAILED_CHECKS emission path -> clean block, no abort. (5) Suite: make test-all 22 specs/633 assertions/0 fail; vbs spec 65/0 incl vbs-llh18-miss (4 assertions); escalation-binding 51/0 byte-stable (SUITE_REUSED META @ L974); qa-gate 74/0 incl bypass-still-binds; P0 META load-bearing (independent mutant: hash-match stripped -> forged label RELEASES); make lint + shellcheck clean. The 14 e2e _fixture-script-sync failures are DRIFT-ONLY (7 fixtures x {qa-gate.sh, verify-before-stop.sh} = the 2 scripts llh.18 advanced; guard message "has DRIFTED from canonical — re-sync fixtures"); 126/140 unit tests pass; out of scope per mandate, not blocking. (6) Residual honest: hand-forged-comment boundary documented at producer (qa-gate.sh:730 THREAT-MODEL BOUNDARY), consumer (vbs:1119 emitted block reason "not a cryptographic sandbox"), and bd notes 349/357 — not overclaimed. [impact-report bypass: gauntlet P0 security fix re-QA; mechanism self-tested]

title: P2: orchestrator can write code via Bash (deny hook + post-edit tracker match only Write|Edit|MultiEdit, not Bash); prevent-orchestrator-edits.sh fails OPEN when orchestrator identity absent/in unprobed field
description: RED-TEAM. orchestrator tool list includes Bash; 'bash -c cat>src/x.ts' is neither denied (settings.json matcher ^(Write|Edit|MultiEdit)$) nor tracked by post-edit.sh → bypasses role guard AND QA change-set. Also prevent-orchestrator-edits.sh probes 7 hardcoded identity fields and fails open ({}) when none carry 'orchestrator'. Fix: fail CLOSED for write-shaped Bash from orchestrator, OR add Bash command-content detection (redirections/tee/sed -i into source) to tracking; tighten identity probe to fail-closed for write tools. Breaks R30/P1 'structurally impossible'.
close_reason: reverted; breaks specialists in identity-less runtime; Bash-vector documented as PROVEN-WITH-CAVEAT residual

comments.author: username
comments.text:
## RED evidence (reproduced before fix) — llh.19

Gate-entered. Reproduced all three sub-findings by driving the hooks with crafted PreToolUse/PostToolUse payloads.

**Finding 1 — orchestrator Bash-write bypasses the role guard.**
- `settings.json` PreToolUse matcher is `^(Write|Edit|MultiEdit)$`. `Bash` is NOT in the matcher, so an orchestrator `bash -c 'cat > src/x.ts <<EOF…'` never reaches `prevent-orchestrator-edits.sh`.
- Even when the hook IS fed a write-shaped Bash payload with `subagent_name=orchestrator`, it returns `{}` (ALLOW) — the script has no Bash-command awareness; it assumed the matcher only ever delivers a write tool.

**Finding 2 — post-edit.sh does not track Bash-writes.**
- `post-edit.sh` extracts `.tool_input.file_path // .tool_input.path`. A Bash command payload has neither, so it emits `{}` and `changed-files.txt` stays absent. The orchestrator's (or any) Bash-write is invisible to the QA change-set.

**Finding 3 — prevent-orchestrator-edits.sh fails OPEN on ambiguous identity.**
- Write tool with NO identity field present → `{}` (ALLOW).
- Write tool with orchestrator identity in an unprobed field (`.meta.agent_role`) → `{}` (ALLOW).
- The 7-field probe + exact-match-list means any orchestrator identity outside those 7 fields, or absent identity, is permitted to write.

All three are now RED. Fix + boundary doc next.

comments.author: username
comments.text:
## FIX applied (NOT approved — left for QA) — llh.19

Owned files only: `prevent-orchestrator-edits.sh`, `post-edit.sh`, `settings.json` + their tests; plus 2 stale lines in `docs/HOOKS.md` that described these scripts. Sibling files (verify-before-stop.sh/invariants.ts; README/WORKFLOW) untouched.

### Fix mechanism
1. **settings.json** — both the PreToolUse (deny) and PostToolUse (track) matchers widened `^(Write|Edit|MultiEdit)$` → `^(Write|Edit|MultiEdit|Bash)$` so Bash now reaches both hooks.
2. **prevent-orchestrator-edits.sh** — added `is_bash_write()` heuristic detecting write-shaped Bash (heredoc-to-file, `>`/`>>` into a path, `tee`, `sed -i`, `perl -i`, `dd of=`, `cp`/`mv`/`install` into the tree), with an explicit transient-sink exemption (`/tmp`, `/dev/null`, `/var/tmp`, `/private/tmp`, fd dups like `2>&1`). Decision logic split into two flags:
   - `IS_WRITE_TOOL` (unambiguous Write/Edit/MultiEdit) and `IS_BASH_WRITE` (heuristic).
   - **Confirmed orchestrator** → deny BOTH (write tool + write-shaped Bash).
   - **Unknown identity + write TOOL** → **fail CLOSED** (deny).
   - **Unknown identity + write-shaped Bash (heuristic only)** → ALLOW (anti-overreach — see boundary below).
   - **Specialist / any known non-orchestrator identity** → allow.
3. **post-edit.sh** — added `extract_bash_write_paths()` (mirrors the same shapes/exemptions) so a Bash-laundered edit's target source path(s) are recovered into `changed-files.txt` and become visible to the QA change-set. Refactored the append loop to track 0..n paths; transient sinks and reads produce no entry.

### Threat-model boundary (honest)
- **Primary guard remains the orchestrator's omitted Write/Edit tools.** This hook is defense-in-depth.
- The Bash-write detector is a **raise-the-bar HEURISTIC, not airtight.** A determined orchestrator can obfuscate (base64→sh, operator built from a var, `python -c`/`node -e` writer). We deliberately do NOT chase those — an arms race in a string parser produces false positives that strangle legitimate Bash.
- **Fail-closed is scoped to the unambiguous Write/Edit TOOLS, NOT the Bash heuristic.** Found by **dogfooding**: this plugin's own live hook fired on my edits because this runtime does not surface `subagent_name` for the top-level agent. Widening the matcher to Bash + fail-closing the heuristic bricked ordinary Bash (any command merely *mentioning* `> file` / `sed -i` / `tee` in a quoted arg, grep pattern, or comment was denied). Resolution: fail-closed only on the definite write tools; an unknown-identity write-shaped Bash is allowed. The residual gap (identity-less orchestrator laundering via Bash) is exactly the "determined orchestrator could obfuscate" boundary already declared out of scope.

### Verification (all green, with the live guard in place)
- `prevent-orchestrator-edits.test.sh`: **31/31** (incl. META-test neutralizing `is_bash_write` → orch-Bash-write DENY flips to ALLOW; the 8 write-shapes; 9 anti-overreach legit-Bash; fail-closed for 3 write tools + unprobed-field; the 2 new heuristic-not-fail-closed cases).
- `post-edit-bash-tracking.test.sh`: **9/9** (Bash heredoc/redirect/sed-i tracked; reads/git/`/tmp`/`/dev/null` NOT tracked; envelope still `{}`).
- Regression: `phase5-synthetic-tests.sh` **33/33** (3.4/3.5/3.6 intact); `agent-mcp-tools-parity` **5/5**; `no-nested-spawn-instructions` **10/10**.
- `make lint` exit 0, 0 findings. `node -e JSON.parse(settings.json)` OK.
- Integrated repro post-fix: holes 1b/3a/3b all DENY; orch git/bd + unknown-id Bash-write + specialist write all ALLOW.

Did NOT run full `make test-all` (siblings mid-edit). No commits. Gate left `entered` (rubric-pending) for QA — NOT approved.

comments.author: username
comments.text: REVERTED (not shipped). The fail-closed-for-write-tools assumed 'every specialist carries a probeable identity' (prevent-orchestrator-edits.sh:47) — empirically FALSE in this runtime: Agent B (a devops specialist) had its own Write/Edit DENIED because the runtime doesn't surface subagent_name to the hook. Fail-closed thus breaks legitimate specialist edits (P0-class regression) to close a P2 (orchestrator Bash-write vector). Reverted prevent-orchestrator-edits.sh + post-edit.sh to HEAD and restored settings matcher to ^(Write|Edit|MultiEdit)$. DISPOSITION: P2 Bash-vector is a documented RESIDUAL — the orchestrator's structurally-omitted Write/Edit tools are the airtight primary guard (red-team confirmed); the Bash vector needs runtime-surfaced identity to close mechanically, which this environment doesn't provide. Ledger R30/P1 → PROVEN-WITH-CAVEAT (Write/Edit structurally blocked; Bash-write residual).

title: G2.n6d Mechanical impact-report: impact-report.sh deterministic impact_of over changed-files; approve refuses without artifact; documented bypass; L1/L2/META
notes:
COMPLETED: G2.n6d mechanical impact-report shipped on all 5 surfaces + L1/L2/META tests. NOT approved (per gauntlet instruction); no commits; zero paid model calls (all code-graph interaction via direct stdio JSON-RPC).

ROOT CAUSE (bug protocol): QA made zero impact_of calls across 4 paid live runs because prompt instructions are advisory — the model can (and did) skip them under every prompt strength tried. Evidence: bd show claude-workflow-plugin-n6d; cassettes/seed/ traces; and the pre-fix L2 red run on THIS task (approve returned ok:true/status:approved with NO impact artifact — see the failing-first evidence comment). Fix: the analysis is now a deterministic artifact (impact-report.sh, invoked by qa-gate.sh enter) and approve REFUSES (exit 2) without a fresh one — mechanics, not prompts. The failing-first L2 assertions flipped red (19 fail) -> green (50 pass) on exactly this fix.

SURFACES:
1. NEW .claude/scripts/impact-report.sh — direct stdio JSON-RPC client over .claude/mcp/code-graph-mcp/bin/code-graph-mcp.js (one server process; initialize -> initialized -> tools/call impact_of {file} per changed file). FIFO-held-open stdin (NOT the component spec's fixed sleeps — index build is unbounded). Canonical change set = LC_ALL=C sort -u + post-edit denylist; change_set_hash = sha256 over it; --hash-only mode keeps generator/checker canonicalisation in ONE place. Degradations: bin/node missing or boot-timeout -> server:"absent"+impact:null (artifact ALWAYS written); per-file tool errors recorded ({ok:false,error}), run continues. Budgets: overall 600s, first call 300s (index build), later calls 60s, boot 30s (env-tunable). macOS quirk found live: node never sees FIFO EOF (kqueue) -> explicit SIGTERM after 2s grace; orphan-free (asserted in L1).
2. qa-gate.sh enter -> generate_impact_report (fresh + idempotent re-enter paths; tolerant — enter NEVER fails on it; failures logged to sync-errors.log + per-task .log + WARNING in observations).
3. qa-gate.sh approve -> sentinel-wrapped refusal block (IMPACT-REPORT-REFUSAL BEGIN/END): exit 2 + emit_error_json with error_key in {impact_report_missing, impact_report_invalid, impact_report_unverifiable, impact_report_stale}; every refusal names artifact path + regenerate cmd + bypass. server:"absent" ACCEPTED. Bypass --no-impact-report '<reason>' (reason mandatory) -> approves; reason in approval comment + impact-bypass note in gate JSON. Verified approvals record the matched hash. bd-mcp bd_qa_approve shells out to qa-gate.sh, so the refusal propagates to MCP callers (refusal JSON rides BdError.stdout).
4. qa.md 3a (FIRST ACTION = read the report, impact_of for symbol-level follow-ups; mechanical-artifact paragraph) + packet 6a item 7 (report in grading packet).
5. verify-before-stop.sh QA-delegation checklist cites the artifact path + regenerate cmd instead of asking QA to make per-file calls (impact_of + code-graph tokens preserved for the cue guard).

TESTS: L1 NEW impact-report.test.sh (30 asserts: absent-bin/absent-node/unbootable/live-server/empty; hash correctness vs independent sha256 + --hash-only; per-file tolerance; no orphaned server). L2 qa-gate spec +20 asserts incl. META-TEST (strip sentinel block -> approve succeeds without artifact = refusal is load-bearing). Suites: make lint clean; L1 16/16 files; L2 21/21 specs (479 asserts); guards qa-impact-of-cue 7/7, agent-mcp-tools-parity rc=0, no-nested-spawn rc=0.

DOGFOOD: bash .claude/scripts/impact-report.sh claude-workflow-plugin-llh.2 against THIS repo: 4.2s, server=code-graph, 7 files, qa-gate.sh shows 28 transitive callers (the high-fan-in regression signal QA never had), out-of-project file recorded as per-file error, hash fresh vs --hash-only (cef76d49...).

KEY DECISIONS: refusal placed AFTER the idempotent already-approved short-circuit (re-approve stays a no-op); bypass audit note declared OUTSIDE the sentinels (stripping the refusal cannot strip the audit trail); server field deliberately unchecked at approve (refusal stops SKIPPED analysis, not degraded environments); LC_ALL=C sort pinned (locale-stable hashes across shell/hook contexts).

IN PROGRESS: none — ready for QA. Artifact for this task is fresh as of notes time; if QA's own edits stale it, regenerate: bash .claude/scripts/impact-report.sh claude-workflow-plugin-llh.2

title: P2: bd '✓ Updated issue' banner pollutes Stop-hook stdout before the {} verdict on the approved/F1 close path (jq on whole stdout fails)
description: RED-TEAM. vbs:664,1173 'bd update --status closed' prints to STDOUT (only stderr silenced); hook stdout becomes '✓ Updated issue: ...\n{}'. Matches the documented antipattern (raw text → Claude may ignore the verdict). Fix: route bd close stdout to /dev/null on the close path.

comments.author: username
comments.text:
QA-GATE work note (specialist, gate-entered).

FIX (evidence-before-fix; repro RED → fix → GREEN):
- Root cause confirmed: on the approved/F1 close path, `bd update <id> --status closed 2>/dev/null` silences only stderr. `bd update` prints `✓ Updated issue: <id>` to STDOUT on success, so the hook stdout became `✓ Updated issue: <id>\n{}` — `jq -e .` over the whole stdout FAILS (the documented "raw text → Claude ignores the verdict" antipattern).
- Repro RED (direct, before fix): approved-path Stop hook stdout = `✓ Updated issue: component-fixture...\n{}`; `jq -e .` over whole stdout failed; `grep 'Updated issue'` matched.
- Fix: routed STDOUT to /dev/null too at BOTH close paths in verify-before-stop.sh:
  - F1 fast-path close (now line 725): `bd update "$CURRENT_TASK" --status closed >/dev/null 2>&1 || log_sync_error ...`
  - approved-flow close (now line 1326): same.
  The `|| log_sync_error` fallback on genuine non-zero exit is preserved unchanged.
- Repro GREEN (after fix): approved-path stdout = exactly `{}`; valid JSON; no banner.

SPEC (failing-first, load-bearing): added case 6a to .claude/tests/component/specs/verify-before-stop.sh — captures STDOUT ONLY (2>/dev/null, NO `tail -1` salvage) on a fresh approved task and asserts (1) whole stdout is valid JSON, (2) no `Updated issue` banner, (3) stdout == `{}`. Against the pre-fix string `✓ Updated issue: …\n{}` all three assertions fail (load-bearing).

COLLATERAL (in-scope, my file): my fix added 8 comment lines above the `lint_rc" -ne 0` guard, shifting it 882→890 and un-landing the existing line-882-anchored lint-wording META-TEST in the same spec. Converted that META-TEST to pattern-anchoring (`/lint_rc" -ne 0/`, occurs once) — same mutation, identical force, drift-proof. Mirrors the already-pattern-anchored truncation META-TEST.

Verify: verify-before-stop component spec green at 68 assertions (65 baseline + 3 new llh.20). NOT approving; no commit.

Verified BOTH bd-close paths now use >/dev/null 2>&1: F1 fast-path (vbs:743) and approved-flow (vbs:1344); both preserve || log_sync_error. Independently reproduced in a live git+beads sandbox: bd update --status closed emits "✓ Updated issue: <id>" to STDOUT (real pollution source); the FIXED hook approved-path stdout = exactly {} (valid JSON); a deliberately-reverted copy (bare 2>/dev/null) emits "✓ Updated issue: <id>\n{}" which fails jq -e (pollution confirmed). comments.author: username
comments.text:
PROGRESS (llh.21, docs-only): Both red-team claims confirmed FALSE against code, then rewritten to match reality. Gate-entered; NOT approved; no commits.

R6 (README.md:20) "Bypass attempts are visible in the hook log" — FALSE. Code evidence: `sync-errors.log` is written only via `log_sync_error`; every call site (grep `.claude/scripts/*.sh`) records best-effort CALL FAILURES / benign status traces, never an attempted bypass. The label-forge bypass (`bd label add <task> qa-approved`, skipping `qa-gate.sh approve`) touches no logging path → no sync-errors.log entry; `verify-before-stop.sh` writes no audit/bypass record on block or release. REWRITTEN (README.md:19-23) to the TRUE adjacent mechanism: the label alone does not release the gate — the Stop hook requires an approval comment whose `change_set_hash` matches the current diff (`verify-before-stop.sh:1037+` release predicate; `task_has_matching_approval_record` at `:420-440`), so a forged label fails. No "hook log" promise remains. Disposition: rewritten (ledger row → REMOVED per vocab: false claim rewritten in docs).

W3 (docs/WORKFLOW.md:420-422) "Ctrl+C interrupt is tracked and visible" — FALSE. No Beads label, no comment, no sync-errors.log entry. (Brief cited vbs:490-492; current tree has it at :543-545 — substance identical.) title: label-milestones: qa-pending milestone declared but not produced as an add-event by the current workflow — decl-vs-flow mismatch (3rd live run affected)
description:
DISCOVERED in the paid node-react-auth live run (trace .claude/tests/e2e/cassettes/replays/node-react-auth-2026-06-13T16-52-03-909Z.jsonl). The `label-milestones` invariant FAILED: declared order [qa-pending, qa-approved], but the derived add-event stream contained NO qa-pending add — observed adds [qa-gate-entered, rubric-pending, ... , qa-approved], net-diff adds [backend, feature, frontend, qa-approved].

This is the 3rd live run where the qa-pending milestone vs the produced add-event stream diverged (runs 3/4 were the 9ke seed traces; llh.4 fixed those shapes — `bd create -l backend,qa-pending` literal form. This run regressed via a NEW command shape llh.4's deriver doesn't map).

INVESTIGATION REQUIRED (state on task with evidence) — (a) workflow gap / (b) deriver gap / (c) stale decl. Evidence-before-fix. Do NOT approve in this session; no commits; no paid runs (offline re-eval only).

File surface: lib/labelEvents.ts (if deriver), lib/invariants.ts (only if invariant logic wrong), fixtures/*/fixture.yaml (if decl), _label-events.unit.spec.ts / _invariants.unit.spec.ts.

comments.author: username
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE = (b) DERIVER GAP — variable-indirected `bd create`. Evidence-before-fix.

## Evidence chain

1. The trace DOES set qa-pending at task creation (so case (a) "workflow never sets it" is FALSE, and case (c) "stale decl" is FALSE — qa-pending is a real, current milestone). The orchestrator created the two child tasks with:
```
BD=./.claude/bin/bd
BE=$("$BD" create "Backend: POST /auth/login with JWT issuance" -t feature -p 2 \
  -l backend,feature,qa-pending \
  --description "..." --json | jq -r '.id')
FE=$("$BD" create "Frontend: LoginForm component" -t feature -p 2 \
  -l frontend,feature,qa-pending \
  --description "..." --json | jq -r '.id')
```
(BD= assignment + `"$BD" create … -l …,qa-pending` confirmed at lines 4/6/11/18 of the trace's flattened Bash command stream. The fixture provisions a local bd shim at .claude/bin/bd; the model aliased it as $BD and invoked through the variable.)

2. The deriver (lib/labelEvents.ts) only matches a LITERAL `bd` token before `create`:
```
BD_CREATE_RE = /\bbd(?:\s+-{1,2}\w[\w-]*(?:=\S+)?)*\s+create\b/g
```
`"$BD" create` has no literal `bd` word, so BD_CREATE_RE never matches → the `-l backend,feature,qa-pending` slice is never scanned → ZERO qa-pending add-events derived. The SAME blind spot exists for BD_LABEL_RE / BD_UPDATE_RE (all anchored on `\bbd`), but only `create` carried qa-pending in this run.

   Contrast: the later `bd update …`, `bd comment …` calls in the SAME run used the literal `bd` (matched fine) — proving the gap is specifically the variable-indirected invocations, of which only the two creates carried a label.

3. OFFLINE RE-EVAL (BEFORE fix) — ran deriveBeadsLabelEvents + INVARIANTS['label-milestones'] against the real recorded trace via a throwaway vitest spec:
   - DERIVED add labels: ["qa-gate-entered","rubric-pending","qa-approved"] — qa-pending ADD count = 0
   - label-milestones [qa-pending, qa-approved]: pass=false, skipped=undefined
   - detail: "missing milestone label add(s): [qa-pending] — declared order: [qa-pending, qa-approved]; observed add events (in order): [qa-gate-entered, rubric-pending, …, qa-approved]; net-diff adds: [backend, feature, frontend, qa-approved]"
   This reproduces the paid-run FAIL exactly (same observed adds, same net-diff adds as the reported evidence).

## Why this is NOT (a) or (c)
- NOT (a) workflow gap: qa-pending IS produced — via creation labels (`-l …,qa-pending`). The flow does set it; the deriver just can't see the variable-indirected form. (Confirmed there is no separate `bd label add qa-pending` / `bd update --add-label qa-pending` anywhere — creation labels are the sole source, exactly as in 9ke run-3.)
- NOT (c) stale decl: qa-pending remains the genuine "queued for QA" milestone that the orchestrator stamps at creation; qa-gate.sh approve still removes it (the remove events ARE in the stream). Rewriting the fixture decl would HIDE a real recorder blind spot, not fix it. The invariant must keep asserting the real qa-pending→qa-approved ordered transition.

## Fix (case-b prescription)
Extend lib/labelEvents.ts so the four `bd …`-anchored Bash scanners ALSO recognize the `$VAR`/`${VAR}` indirection the orchestrator uses for its bd wrapper (BD=./.claude/bin/bd; "$BD" create …). The recorded trace then yields the qa-pending creation-label add → label-milestones PASSES offline on the real trace. Add a unit test for the missed `"$BD" create -l …,qa-pending` shape + a META assertion. No fixture/invariant-logic change (decl + invariant are correct).

comments.author: username
comments.text:
FIX APPLIED + OFFLINE TRACE RE-EVAL (before/after) — root cause = deriver gap.

## Fix (lib/labelEvents.ts) — bd-bound variable indirection
The four `bd`-anchored Bash scanners (label add/remove, update, create) were module-level regexes hard-anchored on a LITERAL `\bbd`. Replaced with PER-COMMAND builders (bdLabelRe / bdUpdateRe / bdCreateRe) that compose a shared head fragment `bdHeadSource(command)`:
- matches literal `bd` OR `$VAR` / `${VAR}` / `"$VAR"` / `"${VAR}"`
- ONLY for var names that the SAME command assigned a bd-ish value (`BD_VAR_ASSIGN_RE`: `NAME=…/bd` | bare `bd` | `$(… bd …)` | backtick subst). Scoping to in-command bd bindings is the over-match guard — an arbitrary `"$FOO" create` is NOT treated as bd.
No change to lib/invariants.ts (invariant logic was correct) and no change to any fixture.yaml (the qa-pending decl is correct — it is a real, current milestone the workflow stamps at creation; rewriting it would HIDE the recorder blind spot, and would weaken a real ordered transition).

## OFFLINE RE-EVAL of the real paid trace (node-react-auth-2026-06-13T16-52-03-909Z.jsonl)
Re-derived events from trace.toolCalls with the fixed deriver, fed into INVARIANTS['label-milestones'] {milestones:[qa-pending,qa-approved]}:

BEFORE (pre-fix / the trace's baked-in beadsLabelEvents field, recorded by the old deriver):
- qa-pending ADD count = 0
- pass=false; detail: "missing milestone label add(s): [qa-pending] — declared order: [qa-pending, qa-approved]; observed add events (in order): [qa-gate-entered, rubric-pending, …, qa-approved]; net-diff adds: [backend, feature, frontend, qa-approved]"

AFTER (re-derived from the SAME recorded toolCalls with the fix):
- qa-pending ADD events now present (2, both taskId "" — the two `"$BD" create -l …,qa-pending` children), at event[2] and event[5]
- pass=TRUE, skipped=false; detail: "all 2 milestone label add(s) proven: [qa-pending@event[2], qa-approved@event[14]] (@event[i] = ordered event-stream match…)"
- Proven as a REAL ordered transition (qa-pending add precedes qa-approved add in-stream), NOT a vacuous/net-diff rescue.

This proves the fix on the real recorded trace with ZERO paid re-run.

## Tests added (_label-events.unit.spec.ts)
