title: Phase 6b: hooks, integrations, doc convention
notes:
COMPLETED: 3 high-sev defects in bd-github-link.sh fixed + new test fixture .claude/scripts/tests/bd-github-link.test.sh (20/20 passing).

DEFECT 1 (claude-workflow-plugin-7o7) — pr-create regex extended on lines 320 (manual) and 416 (hook). Single-alternation regex; BASH_REMATCH[2] is the ref regardless of form. URL sub-capture (issues|pull) is BASH_REMATCH[3] and ignored. Bash ERE has no non-capturing groups so the extra group is unavoidable.

DEFECT 2 (claude-workflow-plugin-vhm) — bold markers removed from gh_link_comment_body line 221 so the body byte-aligns with the grep -F idempotency pattern on line 240. Added a comment header explaining the byte-alignment invariant. Stateful gh stub in the test fixture proves end-to-end '3 close fires => exactly 1 post'.

DEFECT 3 (claude-workflow-plugin-68n) — three-alt regex replaced with extract_tid_from_tail() helper. Handles --status closed (space) AND --status=closed (equals), alt flag-order, multi-id (first wins), --reason="x" before tid, surrounding quotes, shell redirections. Verified against 10 shapes including the canonical 'bd update <tid> --status closed' used by verify-before-stop.sh.

Test fixture: 20 assertions, no real gh calls (PATH-shimmed stub records argv to a log; bd shimmed via --no-daemon to avoid daemon-autostart races on tempdir databases). Phase 5 synthetic tests still pass (29/29). Ready for QA re-review.

comments.author: username
comments.text:
QA-GATE BLOCKED at 2026-05-09T11:16:58Z: QA blocked: 3 high-severity defects in I3 (bd-github-link.sh) discovered during Phase 6b review. All three break the auto-link I3 round-trip in some shape, in different ways:

DEFECT-1 (claude-workflow-plugin-7o7): pr-create regex on lines 320-322 (manual mode) and 416 (hook mode) does NOT match 'Closes https://github.com/x/y/issues/3' URL form. Phase 6b acceptance explicitly required all three formats; only #N and owner/repo#N work today.

DEFECT-2 (claude-workflow-plugin-vhm): post_link_comment() idempotency grep on line 234 looks for 'bd-link: tracked as `<tid>`' but the actual posted body on line 221 wraps the id in bold markers ('**`<tid>`**'). The grep never finds the existing comment, so every re-fire posts a duplicate. Idempotency is broken.

DEFECT-3 (claude-workflow-plugin-68n): hook regex on lines 357-359 fails to match the canonical 'bd update <tid> --status closed' command shape (the '.+' between tid and --status requires non-empty content; a single space is rejected). Worse, the alt-order variant captures the wrong BASH_REMATCH group when it does match. The plugin's own verify-before-stop.sh uses this exact canonical form, so when downstream orchestrator/QA shells out via the same form, the hook silently no-ops.

PASSED checks (no defects):
- I8 cross-repo block path: works correctly with three recovery options, no labels touched.
- I8 same-repo and pre-I8-schema fall-through: degrades silently.
- Hook double-fire check: matchers are anchored and mutually exclusive.
- bd-mcp + code-context-mcp smoke: initialize + tools/list both green; tools return structured data with llm_observations per principle #9.
- Manifest parity: .mcp.json and .claude-plugin/plugin.json mcpServers blocks identical.
- plugin.json paths: agents, hooks, commands, skills, mcp launchers all resolve.
- README convention: bd-mcp/README.md (62 lines) and code-context-mcp/README.md (61 lines), both well under 120; sections in order.
- CONTRIBUTING.md Multi-repo workflows section: present, covers I8 logic and three recovery options.
- Internal MCP tests: 29/29 + 9/9 pass.

The three I3 defects are scoped to bd-github-link.sh, all three are regex/string-matching bugs, all three have clear suggested fixes in their child Beads tasks. Other Phase 6b deliverables (I8, MCP servers, doc convention, manifest parity) are clean.

Three sub-tasks filed under epic claude-workflow-plugin-y4a with discovered-from:claude-workflow-plugin-y4a.13. Re-route to @devops for fixes.

title: Phase 1: Correctness & Safety fixes
notes:
COMPLETED: Phase 1 correctness/safety fixes (13 items: B1/D1/J2 + B5/B6/B8/B9/B10/B11/B12/B13/B14/D4/B16/B17/D7).

Key changes:
- Created .claude/scripts/qa-gate.sh (enter/status/approve/block subcommands; atomic; label-only).
- Removed .qa-tracking/approved marker bypass (B1/D1/J2) and comment-text fallback (B13).
- Replaced narrow extension allowlist with denylist (B6) in post-edit.sh and verify-before-stop.sh — covers node_modules/dist/build/coverage/.git/.next/.nuxt/target/__pycache__, lockfiles (package-lock.json, yarn.lock, pnpm-lock.yaml, Cargo.lock, poetry.lock, go.sum, bun.lockb), .map, .min.js/.min.css, .pyc.
- Fixed B8 placeholder bug — emits <TASK_ID_NEEDED> with recovery instructions.
- post-edit.sh emits valid JSON envelope (B5: {} simplest valid response), race-safe append using flock when available + sort -u on read otherwise (B9).
- intent-router.sh drops length heuristic (B12); skips only on bare slash command or short ack (ok/thanks/yes/no/sure/cool/nice/hmm/got it/sounds good).
- session-end.sh cd guarded; bd sync failures logged to .qa-tracking/sync-errors.log (B11).
- session-start.sh surfaces sync-error log as workflow warning then truncates; B10 edit-count reset preserved; B16 truncation signals on bd blocked / qa-pending output; D7 CLAUDE.md framed as data with explicit preamble.
- hooks.json + settings.json matcher anchored to ^(Write|Edit|MultiEdit)$ (B17).
- settings.json deny rules removed; allow broadened to Read/Write/Edit/MultiEdit/Glob/Grep/LS/Bash/Task/WebFetch/WebSearch (B14/D4).
- All scripts pass bash -n. JSON files validated with jq.
- Synthetic tests run end-to-end: marker bypass no longer works, qa-gate approve clears verify-before-stop block, all denylist patterns honored, dedup race-safe, intent-router skip/inject decisions correct.

KEY DECISIONS:
1. post-edit.sh emits {} (simplest valid envelope) per B5 'simplest valid response' guidance — surfaced review context lives in Stop hook only.
2. flock fallback (no flock on macOS by default) accepts dupes and relies on sort -u at read time, matching the plan's 'No user prompts' constraint.
3. qa-gate.sh handles bd show JSON returning either object or 1-element array (compat across bd versions).
4. qa-gate.sh prefers 'bd comments add' (newer) and falls back to 'bd comment add'.
5. Hardened all bd-output integer comparisons against empty strings (set -e + jq on empty input edge cases).

IN PROGRESS: None — ready for QA. Phase 4 will redesign the gate further (per-task tracking, polyglot test runner, decision gate, etc.); marker-bypass removal and atomic approve are the foundation for that.

title: Phase 2: Agent definitions cleanup
notes:
COMPLETED: Phase 2 (Agent definitions cleanup). All 7 items done:
- C1: Removed Write/Edit from orchestrator. Bash unrestricted per principle #7.
- C2: Added AskUserQuestion to orchestrator tool list.
- C3: Added explicit task discovery to qa.md (bd list --label qa-pending --status open --json) and migrated approve/block flows to bash .claude/scripts/qa-gate.sh helper. AskUserQuestion added to qa tools.
- C4: Specialists (qa, backend, frontend, devops) keep broad tool set: Read, Glob, Grep, LS, Bash, Write, Edit, MultiEdit, Task, WebFetch, WebSearch, AskUserQuestion. No narrowing.
- C5: 'proactive' implemented per Claude Code spec — there is no proactive boolean field. The documented pattern is to embed 'Use proactively...' phrases in the description field (intent-based, not keyword-based). Confirmed via https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/sub-agents.md. All 5 agents now have 'Use proactively' triggers in their description.
- C6: Stripped emoji and ALL CAPS from prompts. Em dashes preserved as proper punctuation. Beads-note prefixes (COMPLETED/IN PROGRESS/KEY DECISIONS) preserved inside code blocks where they serve as informational tokens.
- C7: backend/frontend/devops each gained a 'What QA will test' section with concrete role-specific examples and a 'Design for testability' close.

VALIDATED: All 5 frontmatter blocks parse as valid YAML (verified with PyYAML). qa-pending and qa-gate-entered labels preserved on this task.

IN PROGRESS: None — ready for QA review.

KEY DECISIONS:
- Used 'Use proactively' description-embedded pattern (the actual Claude Code spec) rather than fabricating a 'proactive: true' field that doesn't exist in the documented frontmatter schema (name, description, tools, disallowedTools, model, permissionMode, maxTurns, skills, mcpServers, hooks, memory, background, effort, isolation, color, initialPrompt). This matches both principle #5 (intent in natural language) and principle #8 (proactive everywhere).
- Kept Task tool name (alias for Agent since 2.1.63) for consistency with existing prompt examples like Task('@backend', '...').
- Out of scope (Phase 3): OWASP/6-section structure, performance metrics + RSC matrix, 8-module security taxonomy, 5-step root-cause framework, structured completion contract.

title: Phase 3.frontend: J25 enrichment (perf budget, RSC matrix, a11y)
notes:
COMPLETED: J25 frontend.md enrichment — performance budget, dependency-swap table, RSC matrix, CWV+a11y checklist, component architecture, WebFetch self-check, completion contract.
IN PROGRESS: None — ready for QA.
KEY DECISIONS:
 - Bundle target stated as 'initial JS <200 KB gzipped' with explicit route-level chunking expectation, rather than a single global cap, to be honest about real apps.
 - Performance budget framed as non-negotiable defaults that require a documented decision to deviate, mirrored into the completion contract decisions array.
 - 'use client' guidance written as 'leaf islands' rather than top-down — explicitly warns against marking a parent client to enable a single button.
 - Server-vs-client *state* distinction added alongside the RSC matrix; they are easy to conflate and the agent should pick deliberately.
 - WebFetch self-check section added as its own short H2 so it is structurally callable, not buried in a checklist.

comments.text:
QA-GATE BLOCKED at 2026-05-08T14:42:12Z: J26 and J27 work is sound — but the F7 completion contract in qa.md deviates from the base schema established in backend.md and frontend.md, and the cross-review brief explicitly calls schema drift the most important consistency check.

Backend.md and frontend.md both ship the identical base schema:
  task_id, files_changed, tests_added, decisions, blockers, llm_observations

qa.md ships:
  task_id, approved, files_verified, issues_found, must_fix, suggested_followups, synthetic_tests_run, llm_observations

The brief stated: "QA contract may have additional fields ... but the base 6 fields must be identical across backend / frontend, and qas superset must include the same base." It listed renaming blockers → gating_issues as exactly the kind of deviation that warrants a block. The qa.md contract:
  - renames files_changed → files_verified
  - renames tests_added → synthetic_tests_run
  - drops decisions
  - drops blockers
That is four base-field deviations.

Must fix: restructure section 8 so the base six fields appear with their canonical names and semantics (with QA-specific notes — files_changed = files materially edited as part of the review, tests_added = synthetic tests authored, decisions = calls QA made during the gate, blockers = issues that triggered the block), and then layer the QA-specific fields (approved, issues_found, must_fix, suggested_followups, synthetic_tests_run) on top as the documented superset. synthetic_tests_run can stay alongside tests_added, since they capture different things.

What did pass: 8-module taxonomy (SECRETS → INJECTION → AUTH → CONFIG → DEPS → AI → MOBILE → DATA) is present and correctly intent-routed (line 71 reads the diff as a human would, not regex over filenames). Each module has both "look for" and "Fix" lines and they are concrete. Root-cause framework has all five steps in the right order (Capture, Reproduce, Isolate, Minimal fix, Verify and prevent) and is positioned as the prerequisite for qa-gate.sh block (line 99 and the closing line of section 5). WebFetch self-check sits in the gate-header at line 19. Frontmatter intact, code fences balanced, no emoji, no ALL CAPS prose (only legitimate module-name tokens and acronyms). C6 preserved.

Once the contract is restructured, re-enter the gate.
